title: Optimize binary search start point using Newton's method integer sqrt for recent-biased lookups
trigger_patterns: upper bound search in append-only array
description: Standard binary search starts at the midpoint. For append-only arrays queried mostly for recent values, starting at h - sqrt(h) is far more efficient. Newton's method computes isqrt(h) in ~6 inline division iterations. For a 1000-entry array, this jumps to index ~968 instead of 500, reducing iterations from ~10 to ~2 for recent queries.
pattern_before:
// Standard — always starts at middle
uint256 mid = (low + high) / 2;
when_to_apply: Binary search over append-only sorted arrays where the most recent entries are queried most frequently
when_not_to_apply: Arrays queried uniformly, arrays smaller than 6 entries (overhead exceeds benefit), mutable arrays
